name: Colchis
description: Colchis, located on the eastern coast of the Black Sea (present-day Georgia), held a prominent position in Greek mythology, primarily as the destination of Jason and the Argonauts in their quest for the Golden Fleece. It was a land shrouded in mystery and perceived as being at the edge of the known world, a place of exotic customs and formidable dangers. Ruled by King Aeëtes, son of Helios, the sun god, Colchis was a kingdom of immense wealth and power, guarding the Golden Fleece within a sacred grove dedicated to Ares, the god of war. The land was often depicted as untamed and wild, teeming with fantastical creatures and guarded by a fierce dragon. The Colchians themselves were portrayed as skilled warriors and practitioners of magic. The pursuit of the Golden Fleece in Colchis represents a pivotal moment in Greek heroic narrative, embodying themes of adventure, courage, and the clash between Greek civilization and the perceived barbarity of the 'outer' world. Medea, daughter of Aeëtes, played a crucial role in assisting Jason, using her magical abilities to overcome obstacles and ultimately betray her own family and kingdom for love. The story of Colchis is deeply intertwined with themes of betrayal, ambition, and the consequences of seeking extraordinary treasures. The land's remoteness and its association with magic contributed to its enduring mystique in Greek imagination, solidifying its place as a legendary and perilous realm.
subtitle: Land of the Golden Fleece

keyMyths.title: Jason and the Argonauts
keyMyths.description: Jason and the Argonauts sailed to Colchis to retrieve the Golden Fleece, facing numerous trials and tribulations. Medea, daughter of King Aeëtes, aided Jason with her magical abilities.

keyMyths.title: Medea's Betrayal
keyMyths.description: Medea betrayed her father and her kingdom to help Jason, using her magic to overcome obstacles and ultimately flee with him. Her actions had lasting consequences for both Colchis and herself.

sources.author: Apollonius Rhodius
sources.description: A detailed account of Jason's quest for the Golden Fleece.
sources.text: A detailed account of Jason's quest for the Golden Fleece.

sources.author: Euripides
sources.description: A tragic play focusing on Medea's betrayal and revenge.
sources.text: A tragic play focusing on Medea's betrayal and revenge.
